intake.user_request: Triage the 30 failing/skipped tests: distinguish flaky from known-gap from regression. Fix regressions; document known-gaps.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Triage and targeted fix — no architectural change, no new features. Single investigation pass + targeted fixes. Stub category: test → light.

triage_findings.note: The stub premise '30 failures' was a misread of the vitest summary. The 30 are describe.skip blocks — all intentional, pre-existing infrastructure skips. There is 1 flaky test that appears occasionally.

chroma-store.reason: describe.skip — Chroma requires a live Chroma server; intentional infrastructure skip

pinecone-store.reason: describe.skip — Pinecone requires API key and live index; intentional infrastructure skip

ollama-provider-unreachable.reason: describe.skip — Ollama daemon required; intentional; second block is marked 'Manual' test

scanner-export-recursive.error: ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find module scanner.js imported from scanner-worker.ts
scanner-export-recursive.pattern: Parallel worker race — scanner-worker.ts tries to import scanner.js from src/ but the worker spawns before the module resolves in a parallel test run. Passes when run in isolation.

in_scope: Diagnose and fix the 1 flaky scanner-export test (parallel worker race condition)
Verify all 30 skipped tests have accurate skip comments referencing why they are skipped
Add a stub or comment to each skip block if none exists, so future readers understand the gate condition
Update STUB-FAILING-TESTS-TRIAGE-001 to reflect corrected understanding
out_of_scope: Enabling Chroma/Pinecone/Ollama tests — they require external infrastructure not in scope
Writing new test coverage beyond fixing the flaky test

constraints: Do not remove describe.skip without providing the infrastructure that the tests require
Do not weaken the flaky test's assertions — fix the race condition
Do not increase total skip count — 30 is the baseline
